description: Scaffold libaipm-engine-spec crate and register workspace dependencies
steps: Create crates/libaipm-engine-spec/ directory with Cargo.toml declaring name='libaipm-engine-spec', edition='2021'
Create empty src/lib.rs, src/types.rs, src/helpers.rs, src/generated.rs, build.rs, bin/export-schema.rs files as stubs
Add 'crates/libaipm-engine-spec' to workspace.members in /workspaces/aipm/Cargo.toml
Add phf='0.13', phf_codegen='0.13', schemars='1', jsonschema='0.46', prettyplease='0.2', bitflags='2' to workspace.dependencies in /workspaces/aipm/Cargo.toml
Set crate's [build-dependencies]: serde, serde_json, schemars, jsonschema, phf_codegen, prettyplease, syn, proc-macro2, quote
Set crate's [dependencies]: serde, serde_json, phf, bitflags
Verify cargo build --workspace succeeds with empty stubs

description: Define canonical Rust types in libaipm-engine-spec/src/types.rs with schemars derives
steps: Define EngineApiSchemaFile struct with $schema, meta_schema_version, generated_at, engines, versions, apis, tool_compatibility, suggestions fields

Define EngineBootstrap, EngineApi, ManifestFieldSpec, Constraints, ConventionFile, SizeLimits, ToolCall, HookEvent, FeatureSpec, FeatureKind, ToolCompatibility, ToolSupport, EngineSuggestions structs/enums per spec section 5.2
Apply #[derive(serde::Deserialize, serde::Serialize, schemars::JsonSchema, Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)] to all types
Apply #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] to all named structs
Add pub const META_SCHEMA_VERSION: &str = "1.0.0" at crate root
Use BTreeMap (not HashMap) where the schema requires deterministic key order
Verify cargo build --workspace succeeds

description: Implement bin/export-schema.rs to regenerate schemas/engine-api.schema.json
steps: Implement main() returning Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>
Call schemars::schema_for!(EngineApiSchemaFile) to derive a RootSchema
Serialize to pretty-printed JSON via serde_json::to_string_pretty
Write to ../../schemas/engine-api.schema.json (relative to the crate root)
Verify cargo run -p libaipm-engine-spec --bin export-schema succeeds
Confirm schemas/engine-api.schema.json is created and contains a valid JSON Schema draft 2020-12 document

description: Migrate engine-api-schema.json data file into the new crate with restructured fields
steps: git mv research/engine-api-schema.json crates/libaipm-engine-spec/data/engine-api-schema.json
git mv research/engine-api-changelog.md crates/libaipm-engine-spec/data/engine-api-changelog.md
Add top-level "$schema": "../../../schemas/engine-api.schema.json" and "meta_schema_version": "1.0.0" to the moved JSON

Restructure each entry of apis.<engine>.manifest_fields from prose strings into structured [{name, type, required, constraints: {max_length?, regex?, default?}, notes?}] records (use existing prose like 'name (max 64 chars, regex /^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$/)' as input)
Rename apis.<engine>.detection_heuristics → detection_notes; discovery_algorithm → discovery_notes; rules → rule_notes (per Q3a)
Add hook_events: [{name, aliases, deprecated, notes}] arrays per engine, populated from existing crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/known_events.rs CLAUDE_EVENTS, COPILOT_EVENTS, COPILOT_LEGACY_MAP
Add features: [{kind, manifest_field?, layout_hint?, capabilities?}] arrays per engine, populated from crates/libaipm/src/make/engine_features.rs CLAUDE_FEATURES and COPILOT_FEATURES
Confirm git status shows the data files moved and the JSON edited

description: Generate initial committed schemas/engine-api.schema.json from Rust types
steps: Run cargo run -p libaipm-engine-spec --bin export-schema
Verify schemas/engine-api.schema.json is committed at workspace root

Open the file and confirm it includes definitions for EngineApiSchemaFile, EngineBootstrap, EngineApi, ManifestFieldSpec, Constraints, ConventionFile, SizeLimits, ToolCall, HookEvent, FeatureSpec, FeatureKind, ToolCompatibility, ToolSupport, EngineSuggestions

description: Implement build.rs validation step (data file vs meta-schema)
steps: In build.rs main() returning Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>: print cargo:rerun-if-changed= for data/engine-api-schema.json, ../../schemas/engine-api.schema.json, src/types.rs, build.rs
Read schemas/engine-api.schema.json and data/engine-api-schema.json from disk
Parse meta-schema as serde_json::Value, build a jsonschema::validator_for(&meta_schema)
Call validator.validate(&data_value); on failure return an Err whose Display includes the failing JSON pointer and remediation hint ('regenerate via bin/export-schema')
Deserialize the data into EngineApiSchemaFile via serde_json::from_value
Verify parsed.meta_schema_version equals types::META_SCHEMA_VERSION; on mismatch return Err
Verify cargo build -p libaipm-engine-spec succeeds

description: build.rs codegen: generated Engine enum and EngineSet bitflags
steps: From parsed.engines, generate pub enum Engine with one PascalCase variant per engine name (claude → Claude, copilot-cli → CopilotCli)
Generate impl Engine: pub const ALL: &'static [Engine], pub const fn name(self) -> &'static str (returns the original name string), pub fn from_name(name: &str) -> Option<Self>
Generate bitflags::bitflags! { pub struct EngineSet: u32 { const CLAUDE = ..., const COPILOT_CLI = ..., const ALL = union } }
Use proc-macro2 + quote to produce TokenStream; format with prettyplease::unparse
Write to OUT_DIR/engine_data.rs
Add include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/engine_data.rs")) in src/generated.rs
Verify cargo build -p libaipm-engine-spec succeeds and Engine + EngineSet are accessible from crate root

description: build.rs codegen: ENGINES const table with EngineSpec struct
steps: Define struct EngineSpec { name: &'static str, package: &'static str, version: &'static str, marker_paths: &'static [&'static str], marketplace_manifest_path: &'static str, manifest_search_paths: &'static [&'static str], settings_paths: &'static [&'static str], folder_conventions: &'static [&'static str], convention_files: &'static [(&'static str, &'static [&'static str])] } in types.rs
From parsed.apis, generate pub const ENGINES: &[(Engine, EngineSpec)] = &[ ... ] populated from each engine's EngineApi fields
marker_paths and marketplace_manifest_path: derive from manifest_search_paths (the schema reports them; this is where the .toml→.json fix vs current Rust takes effect — Q2b 'schema wins')
Verify cargo build succeeds and ENGINES.len() >= 2 in a smoke test

description: build.rs codegen: VALID_TOOLS phf::Set via phf_codegen
steps: Iterate every parsed.apis.<engine>.tool_calls[].name and tool_calls[].aliases into a phf_codegen::Set
Write 'pub static VALID_TOOLS: phf::Set<&\'static str> = ' followed by set.build() to OUT_DIR/valid_tools.rs
Either include the file in src/generated.rs alongside engine_data.rs, or merge into engine_data.rs in build.rs
Verify VALID_TOOLS is non-empty and contains known tools like 'Bash', 'Edit', 'Read', 'browser_navigate'

description: build.rs codegen: TOOL_COMPATIBILITY const table
steps: From parsed.tool_compatibility.shared_tools and engine_exclusive_tools, generate pub const TOOL_COMPATIBILITY: &[(&'static str, EngineSet)] = &[ ... ]
Each shared tool maps to EngineSet::ALL
Each engine_exclusive_tools[name].supported_by maps to a bitflag union of those engines
Verify a shared tool ('bash') maps to EngineSet::ALL and a claude-exclusive tool ('Task') maps to EngineSet::CLAUDE only

description: build.rs codegen: HOOK_EVENTS_BY_ENGINE const table
steps: Define HookEventStatic struct { name: &'static str, aliases: &'static [&'static str], deprecated: bool, notes: Option<&'static str> } in types.rs
From parsed.apis.<engine>.hook_events, generate pub const HOOK_EVENTS_BY_ENGINE: &[(Engine, &'static [HookEventStatic])] = &[ ... ]
Verify Engine::Claude maps to ~27 hook events and Engine::CopilotCli to ~10
Confirm that the legacy aliases (PreToolUse → preToolUse) appear in the aliases array of the canonical event

description: build.rs codegen: FEATURES_BY_ENGINE table and FeatureKind generated enum
steps: Decide whether FeatureKind is hand-written in types.rs (already added in feature 2) or generated from parsed kinds; spec section 5.2 has it hand-written so prefer that
Generate pub const FEATURES_BY_ENGINE: &[(Engine, EngineFeatureSet)] = &[ ... ] where EngineFeatureSet is a bitflags struct over FeatureKind variants
Confirm Engine::Claude carries Skill|Agent|Mcp|Hook|OutputStyle and Engine::CopilotCli carries Skill|Agent|Mcp|Hook|OutputStyle|Lsp|Extension

description: build.rs codegen: paths module of centralized path constants
steps: Aggregate all path-constant strings appearing across engines (.claude-plugin, .github/plugin, marketplace.json, marketplace.toml, plugin.json, aipm.toml, settings.json, settings.local.json, .claude, .github, .ai)
Generate pub mod paths { pub const CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR: &str = ".claude-plugin"; pub const GITHUB_PLUGIN_DIR: &str = ".github/plugin"; ... }
Note: aipm.toml is a project file, not engine-specific; include it in paths anyway since it appears alongside the others in scattered literal sites
Verify all spec section 5.3 path constants are present

description: build.rs codegen: constraints module from manifest_fields
steps: Iterate parsed.apis.<engine>.manifest_fields entries; for fields with constraints.max_length, emit named constants
Generate pub mod constraints { pub const PLUGIN_NAME_MAX_LEN: usize = 64; pub const DESCRIPTION_MAX_LEN: usize = 1024; pub const POST_INSTALL_MSG_MAX_LEN: usize = 2048; ... }
If a regex constraint exists, emit pub const PLUGIN_NAME_REGEX: &str = "..." so consumers can compile it (or hand-validate against the same pattern)
Verify that PLUGIN_NAME_MAX_LEN matches the value currently in crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_name_too_long.rs (= 64)

description: Implement libaipm-engine-spec helpers module + unit tests
steps: Implement helpers::is_valid_event(event: &str, engine: Engine) -> bool by scanning HOOK_EVENTS_BY_ENGINE for the engine and checking name + aliases
Implement helpers::suggest_canonical(event: &str, engine: Engine) -> Option<&'static str> by reverse-lookup: find an entry where event matches an alias, return the canonical name
Implement helpers::engine_for_root_dir(name: &str) -> Option<Engine> mapping path component name (e.g. '.claude' → Engine::Claude); pair with marketplace_host_for_root_dir for '.ai'
Implement helpers::valid_tool_name_check(tool: &str, declared: EngineSet) -> Result<(), ToolNameViolation> consulting TOOL_COMPATIBILITY

Add unit tests covering: known events for both engines, legacy alias suggestion (PreToolUse → preToolUse for copilot context), unknown events, root-dir → engine mapping, tool-name check with empty/full/partial declared sets
Verify all helpers compile and pass tests

description: Add libaipm-engine-spec integration tests (validation, drift, generated tables smoke)
steps: tests/schema_validation.rs: load data/engine-api-schema.json + schemas/engine-api.schema.json, run jsonschema validation, assert success

tests/schema_export_drift.rs: run schemars::schema_for!(EngineApiSchemaFile), serialize, semantic-compare against committed schemas/engine-api.schema.json, fail with a hint to re-run export-schema if drift

tests/generated_tables.rs: assert Engine::ALL.len() >= 2, VALID_TOOLS contains 'bash' and 'Bash', TOOL_COMPATIBILITY non-empty, every entry has a non-empty support set, HOOK_EVENTS_BY_ENGINE has entries for each Engine variant
Verify cargo test -p libaipm-engine-spec passes all three tests

description: Wire libaipm to depend on libaipm-engine-spec
steps: Add libaipm-engine-spec = { path = "../libaipm-engine-spec" } to crates/libaipm/Cargo.toml [dependencies]
Add a lib.rs re-export: pub use libaipm_engine_spec::{Engine, EngineSet, MarketplaceHost, paths, constraints} (paths/constraints/MarketplaceHost added in later features)
Verify cargo build -p libaipm succeeds

description: Replace crates/libaipm/src/engine.rs Engine enum with re-export of generated Engine
steps: Delete the local pub enum Engine { Claude, Copilot } at crates/libaipm/src/engine.rs:13-21
Replace const fn marker_paths(self) etc. (lines 25-53) with thin wrappers that read libaipm_engine_spec::ENGINES

Where existing call sites match Engine::Copilot, ensure they continue to compile by either renaming references to Engine::CopilotCli or by adding pub use libaipm_engine_spec::Engine; pub use libaipm_engine_spec::Engine::CopilotCli as Copilot; (alias for backwards compat — defer cleanup if needed)
Update validate_plugin (engine.rs:155-157) to iterate Engine::ALL.iter().map(|e| e.spec().marker_paths)
Update Engine::all_names → use Engine::ALL.iter().map(|e| e.name())
Verify cargo build -p libaipm succeeds and all engine.rs unit tests pass

description: Replace crates/libaipm/src/discovery/types.rs Engine enum + introduce MarketplaceHost
steps: Delete the local pub enum Engine { Claude, Copilot, Ai } at crates/libaipm/src/discovery/types.rs:38-46
Add pub enum MarketplaceHost { Ai } in discovery/types.rs (hand-written, since .ai/ is a marketplace-host concept, not an engine — Q2)
Replace any uses of the old Engine in discovery code with libaipm_engine_spec::Engine; for the Ai variant, use Option<MarketplaceHost>
Update DiscoveredFeature struct to carry both engine: Option<Engine> and host: Option<MarketplaceHost> (or a tagged enum if they're mutually exclusive)
Verify cargo build -p libaipm succeeds

description: Update discovery/source.rs and lint/rules/scan.rs path-walker dispatch
steps: Update infer_engine_root in crates/libaipm/src/discovery/source.rs:37-54 to call libaipm_engine_spec::helpers::engine_for_root_dir for .claude/.github and helpers::marketplace_host_for_root_dir for .ai
Update source_type_from_path in crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/scan.rs:33-44 similarly
Replace literal '.claude'/'.github'/'.ai' strings with libaipm_engine_spec::paths::CLAUDE_DOT, GITHUB_DOT, AI_DOT
Verify discovery and lint tests pass

description: Update discovery/instruction.rs, layout.rs, discovery_legacy.rs to use generated paths and convention files
steps: Replace INSTRUCTION_FILENAMES const at crates/libaipm/src/discovery/instruction.rs:32-39 with a function that returns convention filenames aggregated from libaipm_engine_spec::ENGINES.iter().flat_map(|e| e.1.convention_files.iter().map(|(f, _)| *f)) deduplicated
Update crates/libaipm/src/discovery/layout.rs lines 52, 194, 227 literal substitutions to use libaipm_engine_spec::paths::*
Update crates/libaipm/src/discovery_legacy.rs:48-71 literal directory list to use libaipm_engine_spec::paths::CLAUDE_DOT, GITHUB_DOT
Verify discovery tests pass

description: Delete known_events.rs and reroute hook_unknown_event.rs to engine-spec helpers
steps: Delete crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/known_events.rs entirely
Remove the corresponding mod statement from crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/mod.rs
Update crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/hook_unknown_event.rs:73 to call libaipm_engine_spec::helpers::is_valid_event and helpers::suggest_canonical instead of the local functions
Migrate the test cases from the deleted file into crates/libaipm-engine-spec/src/helpers.rs unit tests if they aren't already covered there
Verify cargo test -p libaipm passes

description: Replace skill name/desc/regex/oversized lint constants with generated constraints
steps: In crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_name_too_long.rs:13 replace const MAX_SKILL_NAME_LENGTH with libaipm_engine_spec::constraints::PLUGIN_NAME_MAX_LEN
In crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_desc_too_long.rs:13 replace const MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH with libaipm_engine_spec::constraints::DESCRIPTION_MAX_LEN

In crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_name_invalid.rs:13-24 replace the byte-iteration regex implementation with a check using libaipm_engine_spec::constraints::PLUGIN_NAME_REGEX (compile via regex crate, or keep a hand-validator and add a test cross-checking against the constant)
If a hand-validator is kept (per OQ-S3), add a test that asserts the validator's behavior matches what the regex pattern would accept
Verify lint tests pass

description: Implement valid-tool-name lint rule (issue #697) and wire it into the lint registry
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/valid_tool_name.rs implementing the Rule trait per spec section 5.6
Read declared engines from the plugin manifest (aipm.toml [engines] block) — if absent, treat as EngineSet::empty()
Iterate referenced tool names in agent/skill/hook frontmatter (look at existing agent_missing_tools.rs and frontmatter.rs for the extraction sites)
For each tool, look up libaipm_engine_spec::TOOL_COMPATIBILITY; if engine-exclusive and declared engines don't cover the support set, emit a Diagnostic per spec
Wire the rule in crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/mod.rs

Add unit tests covering: undeclared + claude-only tool, undeclared + copilot-only tool, declared ["claude"] + claude-only tool (clean), declared ["claude"] + copilot-only tool (error), shared tool (always clean)
Verify cargo test -p libaipm passes

description: Update lint/mod.rs RECOGNIZED_SOURCE_NAMES
steps: At crates/libaipm/src/lint/mod.rs:30 replace const RECOGNIZED_SOURCE_NAMES: &[&str] = &[".claude", ".github", ".ai"] with a function or const constructed from libaipm_engine_spec::paths::CLAUDE_DOT, GITHUB_DOT, AI_DOT
Update all readers of RECOGNIZED_SOURCE_NAMES if its type changes
Verify lint tests pass

description: Update manifest/types.rs engines field to EngineSet and adjust serde
steps: At crates/libaipm/src/manifest/types.rs:66 change pub engines: Option<Vec<String>> to pub engines: Option<EngineSet>
Implement custom serde for EngineSet that reads/writes a JSON array of engine name strings
Update all readers of manifest.package.engines to call .iter() / .contains() on the new EngineSet
Verify manifest unit tests pass; if any test relied on the Vec<String> shape, port it to the new type

description: Update manifest/validate.rs to source name regex and version checks from generated constraints
steps: At crates/libaipm/src/manifest/validate.rs:55, 92-103 replace the hand-rolled regex with the constant from libaipm_engine_spec::constraints::PLUGIN_NAME_REGEX (or keep the byte validator with a cross-check test, per OQ-S3)
Length caps in manifest validators reroute to libaipm_engine_spec::constraints::*
Keep semver::VersionReq::parse and Version::parse usage (validate.rs:118, :179) — versions are not schema-driven
Verify manifest tests pass

description: Rewrite make/engine_features.rs as re-export of generated FeatureKind and FEATURES_BY_ENGINE
steps: Replace pub enum Feature in crates/libaipm/src/make/engine_features.rs:8 with pub use libaipm_engine_spec::FeatureKind as Feature
Delete CLAUDE_FEATURES and COPILOT_FEATURES constants
Replace features_for_engine(engine: &str) signature with features_for_engine(engine: Engine) -> EngineFeatureSet that consults libaipm_engine_spec::FEATURES_BY_ENGINE
Update callers in crates/libaipm/src/make/* to pass Engine values instead of strings
Update crates/aipm/src/main.rs:1074-1077 string match arms ('claude'|'copilot'|'both') to typed Engine + EngineSet logic
Verify make tests pass and the make CLI still accepts the same engine inputs

description: Update migrate/adapters/{agent,hook,skill}.rs to use generated Engine type
steps: In crates/libaipm/src/migrate/adapters/agent.rs lines 33-34, 58, 91-92, ~108: update Engine references (Engine::Copilot becomes Engine::CopilotCli throughout, or keep Copilot alias from earlier feature)
In crates/libaipm/src/migrate/adapters/hook.rs:29 same
In crates/libaipm/src/migrate/adapters/skill.rs lines 26, 76 same
Replace any hardcoded "${COPILOT_AGENT_DIR}/" / "${CLAUDE_AGENT_DIR}/" prefix strings with refs to libaipm_engine_spec::paths or per-engine spec lookup
Verify migrate tests pass

description: Centralize path literals in 13 migrate detector files
steps: For each of crates/libaipm/src/migrate/{agent,command,hook,mcp,skill,output_style}_detector.rs and copilot_{agent,extension,hook,lsp,mcp,skill}_detector.rs: identify literal occurrences of .claude-plugin, .github/plugin, marketplace.json, marketplace.toml, plugin.json, .claude, .github, settings.json, settings.local.json
Replace each with a reference to libaipm_engine_spec::paths::*
Run cargo build -p libaipm and cargo test -p libaipm and ensure no behavior change

description: Centralize scattered path literals in installer, init, acquirer, cache, and other libaipm modules
steps: crates/libaipm/src/installer/manifest_editor.rs lines 104, 123, 137, 151, 166, 200, 212: replace aipm.toml literals with paths::AIPM_TOML
crates/libaipm/src/init.rs lines 61, 286, 296: same
crates/libaipm/src/acquirer.rs lines 220, 331, 432, 471, 497, 587, 596, 714, 756, 802, 951, 994, 1020, 1028, 1071, 1079: replace .claude-plugin, plugin.json, aipm.toml, etc.
crates/libaipm/src/cache.rs lines 534, 1008: same
crates/libaipm/src/manifest/mod.rs lines 513, 523, 531: aipm.toml → paths
crates/libaipm/src/linker/{pipeline,hard_link}.rs: aipm.toml → paths
crates/libaipm/src/migrate/reconciler.rs lines 307, 319: settings.json → paths

Run cargo grep for any remaining stray literals (.claude-plugin|.github/plugin|marketplace.json|marketplace.toml|plugin.json|aipm.toml|CLAUDE.md|AGENTS.md|copilot-instructions.md|GEMINI.md) outside libaipm-engine-spec and confirm intentional remains only
Verify cargo build/test -p libaipm clean

description: Update aipm CLI (wizard, main, wizard_tty) to use generated Engine and paths
steps: crates/aipm/src/wizard.rs:297 replace pub const ENGINE_OPTIONS: &[&str] with a function returning libaipm_engine_spec::Engine::ALL.iter().map(|e| e.name())
crates/aipm/src/main.rs:730 replace const SUPPORTED_SOURCES: &[&str] = &[".claude", ".github", ".ai"] with refs to libaipm_engine_spec::paths::*
crates/aipm/src/main.rs:1074-1077 replace string-typed engine validation with Engine::from_name + EngineSet handling for the 'both' case
crates/aipm/src/wizard_tty.rs:145 replace match engine.as_str() string arms with typed match on Engine
Verify cargo build -p aipm and tests/cli_tests.rs pass

description: Update reverse-binary-analysis.md workflow prompt for new schema fields and paths
steps: Edit .github/workflows/reverse-binary-analysis.md frontmatter safe-outputs.create_pull_request.protected-paths to add 'crates/**', 'Cargo.toml', 'Cargo.lock', 'schemas/**' (Q5a)
Update prompt path references (md:63, :208, :278): research/engine-api-schema.json → crates/libaipm-engine-spec/data/engine-api-schema.json
Update prompt path references (md:65, :256, :278): research/engine-api-changelog.md → crates/libaipm-engine-spec/data/engine-api-changelog.md
Update extraction prompt (md:128-164) to require detection_notes/discovery_notes/rule_notes (renamed) instead of detection_heuristics/discovery_algorithm/rules
Update extraction prompt to emit manifest_fields as structured records with {name, type, required, constraints: {max_length?, regex?, default?}, notes?}
Update extraction prompt to also emit hook_events: [{name, aliases, deprecated, notes}] per engine
Update extraction prompt to also emit features: [{kind, manifest_field?, layout_hint?, capabilities?}] per engine
Update schema-shape JSONC examples (md:74-82, :196-203, :212-254) to reflect $schema URI top-level field and meta_schema_version field
Confirm changes preserve PR title prefix [reverse-binary-analysis] and labels automation, analysis

description: Recompile reverse-binary-analysis.lock.yml via gh aw compile
steps: Run gh aw compile reverse-binary-analysis from the repo root
Verify .github/workflows/reverse-binary-analysis.lock.yml is regenerated
Confirm protected-paths in the .lock.yml matches the .md frontmatter
Confirm the data file path appears correctly in safe-outputs JSON config
Commit both .md and .lock.yml together

description: Update CLAUDE.md to document libaipm-engine-spec and export-schema workflow
steps: Add an entry under Project Structure listing crates/libaipm-engine-spec/
Add a 'Schema export' section explaining cargo run -p libaipm-engine-spec --bin export-schema → schemas/engine-api.schema.json
Add a note that crates/libaipm-engine-spec/build.rs and bin/export-schema.rs should be in the cargo llvm-cov --ignore-filename-regex pattern
Add a note about META_SCHEMA_VERSION bumping when types.rs changes in a breaking way
Update the workflow file table to reflect that reverse-binary-analysis writes to crates/libaipm-engine-spec/data/ instead of research/
Verify CLAUDE.md renders without broken links

description: Add stray-literals grep script and BDD scenarios for valid-tool-name
steps: Create scripts/check-no-stray-literals.sh that greps the workspace for literal occurrences of '.claude-plugin', '.github/plugin', 'marketplace.json', 'marketplace.toml', 'plugin.json', 'aipm.toml' outside crates/libaipm-engine-spec/ and tests/, and reports any hits
Make the script return non-zero on any unexpected hit so it can run in CI

Add at least 3 BDD scenarios in tests/features/lint/ for valid-tool-name: undeclared engines + engine-exclusive tool emits warning, declared engines covering tool support emits no diagnostic, declared engines NOT covering emits error
Verify the BDD scenarios pass via cargo test --test bdd in libaipm

description: Verify workspace cargo build/test/clippy/fmt and 89% branch coverage
steps: Run cargo build --workspace; expect clean exit
Run cargo test --workspace; expect all green (including new schema_validation, schema_export_drift, generated_tables, valid_tool_name unit tests, BDD scenarios)
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings; expect clean (zero warnings)
Run cargo fmt --check; expect clean

Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch followed by cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch with the existing --ignore-filename-regex pattern updated to also ignore crates/libaipm-engine-spec/build.rs and crates/libaipm-engine-spec/src/bin/export-schema.rs
Confirm TOTAL branch column ≥ 89%
If any of the above fails, fix root cause (do NOT add #[allow] or modify ignore patterns to mask failures)
